problem.title: Substring with Concatenation of All Words
problem.description: You are given a string s and an array of strings words. All the strings of words are of the same length.
assumptions: Each word must appear exactly once.
Return starting indices of all valid concatenations.

example.explanation: At index 0: 'barfoo'='bar'+'foo'. At index 9: 'foobar'='foo'+'bar'. Sliding window of size totalLen.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Sliding Window [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: A fixed-length window of size wordLength × numWords slides over s; at each position we check whether the window contains exactly the required multiset of words by extracting fixed-size chunks and comparing against a frequency map. Using two frequency maps — one for required counts and one for the current window — allows O(1) per-word membership and count checks. The key insight is that all words have equal length, so the window can be partitioned into non-overlapping fixed-size slots without ambiguity.
